blocks.content:
Congratulations on completing this journey! Job well done!

We hope you've enjoyed traveling with us as you:

- Learned why exploring Elasticsearch logs in Grafana improves troubleshooting efficiency
- Navigated to the Explore interface and selected your Elasticsearch data source
- Selected the Logs query type for optimized log visualization
- Wrote Lucene queries to search and filter specific log entries
- Configured log display options for proper message and level field mapping
- Filtered logs by time range to focus on specific investigation windows
- Added a log query to a dashboard for ongoing monitoring

---

### More to explore (optional)

The world is your oyster! Read more about how you can:

- [Elasticsearch query editor](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/datasources/elasticsearch/query-editor/)
- [Elasticsearch template variables](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/datasources/elasticsearch/template-variables/)
- [Configure data links](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/datasources/elasticsearch/configure/)

---

### Related paths

Consider taking the following paths after you complete this journey.

- [Explore logs using Logs Drilldown](https://grafana.com/docs/learning-paths/drilldown-logs/)
- [Visualize logs in a Grafana Cloud dashboard](https://grafana.com/docs/learning-paths/visualization-logs/)
